title: tetrastigma andamanicum
intro: Tetrastigma andamanicum, also known as the Andaman five-angled vine, is a type of climber native to the Andaman and Nicobar Islands. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various skin conditions. Tetrastigma andamanicum can be used in herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
